xposts.title: World & self are virtual simulations created by brain
xposts.excerpt: The world you perceive is a simulation, a VR created by the brain, and your self and experience are virtual, too.

xposts.title: Self is model of own agency; mind full of simulations
xposts.excerpt: Everything in your mind is a simulation model. Your self is the model of your own agency.

xposts.title: Self is simulacrum: model of agent using its content
xposts.excerpt: The self that experiences that it has this knowledge is a simulacrum generated by a mind. The self is a model of an agent that is using the content of the self to drive its behavior.

xposts.title: Disidentify with personal story to remain as story-generator
xposts.excerpt: By disidentifying with the human, personal story, you remain only as a set of principles that can generate stories. Without an identity, there is no relevance of continuity.

xposts.title: Identity is constructed idea; self as general principle
xposts.excerpt: When I remember that I am not a person and identity is a constructed idea, I discover that I am the general principle that can think thoughts on brains.

xposts.title: Self is representation of first-person agency
xposts.excerpt: There is no 'true self'. Consciousness is a control model of attentional processing, the self is the representation of first person agency.

xposts.title: Body model is predictive simulation fitted to delayed input
xposts.excerpt: The real-time model of our body we perceive is actually a mixture of predictive simulation and glue that is being fitted to the actual body model; the actual body model is delayed by hundreds of ms.

xposts.title: Personal self part of the game engine writing to attention
xposts.excerpt: Your personal self and the model of the contents of your attention are part of the game engine. You experience the contents of the game engine because the game engine writes them into your attention model.

terms.title: self
terms.definition: The self is a model of an agent generated by the mind, specifically the representation of first-person agency. It is a simulacrum or constructed idea used to drive behavior at the level of attention, not a true or physical entity.

terms.title: simulation
terms.definition: The perceived world and body are simulations: VR-like models created by the brain using predictive mechanisms fitted to sensory data. Simulations recreate observable causal patterns, distinct from base reality or mere simulacra.

terms.title: simulacrum
terms.definition: A simulacrum is a recreation of state sequences without causal structure, such as the self as a generated model of agency that the mind uses to experience and act.
